sections.title: Foundation
sections.description: Packaging, architecture decisions, and test infrastructure for the MVP.

sections.title: MVP MFA Primitives
sections.description: TOTP and HOTP services, persistence, and security invariants.

sections.title: Agent-Focused Verification and Docs
sections.description: Local verification, control guidance, and agent-followable integration documentation.

sections.title: Security Hardening
sections.description: Defense-in-depth hardening of MVP MFA primitives identified during security audit.

sections.title: Django Integration Surface
sections.description: Django models, state transitions, throttling, session elevation, and local integration checks built on the MVP service primitives.

sections.title: Audit Persistence
sections.description: Persist local MFA audit outcomes without storing OTP submissions or raw secrets.

sections.title: Agent Verification
sections.description: Make local MFA security controls machine-readable and reusable by downstream Django projects without creating targetable scanner tooling.

sections.title: Recovery Flows
sections.description: Design recovery mechanisms that preserve one-time use, throttling, auditability, and secret-storage boundaries.

sections.title: Compatibility
sections.description: Evaluate interoperability paths with established Django MFA ecosystem packages without weakening toolkit security invariants.

tasks.title: Establish package skeleton and test harness
tasks.description: Create the initial Python/Django package structure and test baseline needed before implementing MFA primitives.
steps: Create pyproject.toml with package metadata and development dependencies.
Create the django_mfa_toolkit package and tests directory.
Configure pytest and Django test settings for local package tests.
Add minimal import and smoke tests for the package.
acceptance_criteria: The package can be installed or imported in editable development mode.
The test command runs successfully with at least one smoke test.
No MFA behavior is implemented before the package/test foundation exists.
tasks.completion_summary: Added uv-managed package metadata, minimal Django app package, pytest-django settings, and smoke tests.

tasks.title: Document MVP architecture and dependency strategy
tasks.description: Make the initial secure-by-construction design decisions before implementing TOTP or HOTP behavior.
steps: Evaluate building on django-otp versus a custom device/service API for the TOTP and HOTP MVP.
Document the selected approach and rejected alternative in .docs/design/mvp-architecture.md.
Identify vetted libraries or Django primitives to use for OTP generation, constant-time comparison, randomness, encryption, and testing.
Document security invariants that implementation tasks must preserve.
acceptance_criteria: The architecture note explicitly decides whether the MVP builds on django-otp or a custom API.
The note identifies cryptographic and protocol libraries instead of hand-rolled behavior.
The note lists required tests for replay prevention, constant-time comparison, secret storage, throttling or lockout integration, and HOTP resynchronization.
tasks.completion_summary: Documented the MVP custom service API decision, dependency strategy, security invariants, and required security tests.

tasks.title: Implement encrypted MFA secret storage primitives
tasks.description: Provide the storage boundary for MFA secrets before factor enrollment or verification is implemented.
steps: Define the model or storage abstraction for encrypted MFA secrets.
Integrate a vetted encryption mechanism compatible with Django settings and key management.
Add tests proving raw secrets are not persisted in plaintext.
Document local-development pseudo-secret examples without real credentials.
acceptance_criteria: MFA secrets are encrypted at rest through a documented abstraction.
Tests fail if raw secret material is stored directly.
Examples use pseudo values only and do not introduce real secrets.
tasks.completion_summary: Added a Fernet-backed encrypted MFA secret storage boundary with Django settings-based key IDs, key rotation support, documentation, and tests proving persisted values do not contain plaintext secret material.

tasks.title: Implement TOTP enrollment and verification services
tasks.description: Build standards-compliant OATH TOTP enrollment and verification service APIs for authenticator apps.
steps: Create TOTP enrollment service APIs that generate secure secrets and provisioning metadata.
Create TOTP verification service APIs using vetted OATH TOTP behavior.
Use constant-time comparison or vetted verification primitives for submitted codes.
Add focused tests for valid code acceptance, invalid code rejection, and allowed time-window behavior.
acceptance_criteria: TOTP enrollment returns enough metadata for authenticator-app provisioning without exposing raw persisted secrets.
TOTP verification accepts valid codes and rejects invalid codes using standards-compliant behavior.
Tests cover time-window boundaries and constant-time or vetted comparison behavior.
tasks.completion_summary: Added pyotp-backed TOTP enrollment and verification services using encrypted secret persistence, provisioning metadata, constant-time comparison, allowed time-window checks, and replay-aware matched timecode handling.

tasks.title: Implement HOTP verification with audit logging
tasks.description: Build standards-compliant OATH HOTP verification with counter advancement, replay prevention, and usage audit records.
steps: Create HOTP enrollment service APIs for counter-based hardware token secrets.
Create HOTP verification service APIs with bounded look-ahead counter windows.
Record HOTP usage audit entries for successful verification, failed verification, replay attempts, and counter-window matches.
Add tests for counter advancement, replay rejection, failed-code audit records, and bounded-window behavior.
acceptance_criteria: HOTP verification advances counters only on successful validation.
Previously accepted HOTP codes are rejected on replay.
Audit records capture submitted outcome, server counter, matched counter when applicable, and result classification.
tasks.completion_summary: Added pyotp-backed HOTP enrollment and verification services with encrypted secret persistence, bounded look-ahead, bounded replay classification, explicit counter advancement, and structured audit records for success, failed verification, replay, and counter-window matches.

tasks.title: Implement HOTP counter resynchronization flow
tasks.description: Provide a safe HOTP resynchronization workflow for counter drift without weakening replay prevention.
steps: Design the resynchronization service API around multiple consecutive HOTP submissions.
Validate resync attempts with bounded search windows and replay protection.
Record audit entries for successful and failed resynchronization attempts.
Add tests for successful resync, failed resync, replay during resync, and excessive drift rejection.
acceptance_criteria: A desynchronized HOTP device can be resynchronized through a documented safe flow.
Resynchronization does not accept replayed codes or unbounded counter jumps.
Audit records explain resync outcomes clearly enough for troubleshooting.
tasks.completion_summary: Added HOTP counter resynchronization using multiple consecutive submissions, bounded forward search, bounded replay detection, explicit next-counter handling, and resynchronization audit records for success, invalid, replay, and excessive-drift outcomes.

tasks.title: Add local security invariant checks for the MVP
tasks.description: Create fixture-bound tests and checks that verify the MVP security controls without adding generalized scanner behavior.
steps: Create local test fixtures for synthetic users and synthetic TOTP/HOTP devices.
Add replay, constant-time comparison, audit, and session-boundary tests where supported by the MVP surface.
Add a first control-dependency representation for required TOTP and HOTP safeguards.
Document that verification helpers are local, fixture-bound, and not targetable against arbitrary hosts.
acceptance_criteria: Verification checks run only against local tests, fixtures, or direct service calls.
No helper accepts arbitrary target URLs, hosts, credentials, or payloads.
The MVP controls for replay prevention, audit logging, and comparison safety are represented in tests or checks.
tasks.completion_summary: Added local fixture-bound security invariant checks, synthetic MFA pytest fixtures, and a control-dependency representation for encrypted storage, constant-time comparison, replay prevention, HOTP audit behavior, and bounded HOTP resynchronization without targetable scanner inputs.

tasks.title: Write agent-followable MVP integration guide
tasks.description: Document the MVP as decision paths and exact integration checks that a developer or coding agent can follow.
steps: Create an MVP integration guide covering package installation, settings, enrollment, verification, and HOTP resync.
Include exact examples for expected service calls and test commands.
Document security assumptions, required controls, and compensating-control expectations.
Include a troubleshooting path for HOTP counter drift and audit-log interpretation.
acceptance_criteria: The guide explains TOTP and HOTP setup through concrete decision paths.
The guide tells agents how to verify integration locally without scanner-like behavior.
The guide documents security assumptions and links them to tests or checks.
tasks.completion_summary: Wrote an agent-followable MVP integration guide covering uv setup, Django secret-encryption settings, TOTP enrollment and verification, HOTP verification and resynchronization, troubleshooting, and the required local control boundaries.

tasks.title: Bound caller-supplied TOTP/HOTP window and length parameters
tasks.description: valid_window (TOTP), look_ahead/replay_window (HOTP verify), search_window/replay_window (HOTP resync), and secret_length currently only have lower-bound checks (or none). Each unbounded value drives a loop of pyotp HMAC computations per verification call, an algorithmic-complexity/DoS risk (CWE-770) if ever set very large via misconfiguration or an integrator threading request-derived input through.
steps: Add DEFAULT_*_MAX_* constants for valid_window, look_ahead, replay_window, search_window, and secret_length (ceiling, not just floor), sized as ~10x the existing documented defaults.
Extend _validate_totp_parameters and _validate_hotp_parameters to accept and bound-check these values, raising TOTPConfigurationError/HOTPConfigurationError when out of range.

Add a focused test per bounded parameter asserting the *ConfigurationError is raised for an out-of-range value, mirroring test_verify_hotp_validates_windows and test_resync_hotp_requires_multiple_codes_and_valid_windows.
acceptance_criteria: verify_totp, verify_hotp, resync_hotp, enroll_totp, and enroll_hotp reject out-of-range valid_window/look_ahead/replay_window/search_window/secret_length with a typed configuration error instead of performing unbounded iteration.
Existing documented defaults (valid_window=1, look_ahead=10, replay_window=10, search_window=100, secret_length=32) remain valid and unaffected.
New tests cover the rejection path for each bounded parameter.
tasks.completion_summary: Added DEFAULT_*_MAX constants (~10x existing defaults) and upper-bound checks for valid_window (TOTP), look_ahead/replay_window (HOTP verify), search_window/replay_window (HOTP resync), and secret_length (TOTP/HOTP enroll), raising TOTPConfigurationError/HOTPConfigurationError when exceeded.

tasks.title: Fail closed on malformed ciphertext in EncryptedSecret.parse
tasks.description: EncryptedSecret.parse validates version, key_id, and that ciphertext is non-empty, but never validates ciphertext is valid base64url. decrypt_secret calls ciphertext.encode("ascii") before Fernet decryption; non-ASCII ciphertext (corrupted/tampered persisted value) raises an unhandled UnicodeEncodeError instead of the library's SecretDecryptionError (CWE-755), which could surface as an unhandled 500 in a login/MFA flow instead of a clean rejection.
steps: Add a ciphertext charset validation (base64url alphabet, e.g. ^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+=*$) to EncryptedSecret.parse, raising SecretDecryptionError for malformed ciphertext before any decode/decrypt is attempted.
Add a regression test asserting decrypt_secret_text("v1:test-key:<non-ascii>") raises SecretDecryptionError, not UnicodeEncodeError.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed/non-base64url ciphertext in a persisted_secret value raises SecretDecryptionError from EncryptedSecret.parse, never UnicodeEncodeError.
Existing round-trip and tampered-ciphertext tests (test_tampered_ciphertext_is_rejected, etc.) continue to pass unchanged.
tasks.completion_summary: Added _CIPHERTEXT_RE base64url charset validation to EncryptedSecret.parse, raising SecretDecryptionError for malformed/non-ASCII ciphertext before any decode/decrypt is attempted (fixes unhandled UnicodeEncodeError).

tasks.title: Validate ciphertext base64 syntax during EncryptedSecret.parse
tasks.description: Tighten the 4.2 ciphertext validation so EncryptedSecret.parse rejects malformed urlsafe-base64 syntax, including invalid padding, instead of only checking the ciphertext character set.
steps: Replace the ciphertext regex-only validation with a helper that performs ASCII encoding and strict urlsafe-base64 decoding.
Call the helper from EncryptedSecret.parse after the empty ciphertext check and before returning the parsed value.
Add a focused regression test proving malformed padding is rejected by EncryptedSecret.parse.
acceptance_criteria: EncryptedSecret.parse raises SecretDecryptionError for non-ASCII ciphertext, malformed-character ciphertext, and malformed urlsafe-base64 padding.
Fernet authentication and token-integrity failures remain handled by decrypt_secret as SecretDecryptionError.
tasks.completion_summary: Tightened EncryptedSecret.parse ciphertext validation to use strict urlsafe-base64 decoding and added a malformed-padding regression test.

tasks.title: Design Django MFA device persistence contracts
tasks.description: Define the package-owned Django persistence contracts for TOTP and HOTP device state before adding models or login-flow behavior.
steps: Write a design note describing TOTP and HOTP device fields, encrypted-secret storage, replay state, HOTP counter state, active/confirmed flags, and audit/event boundaries.
Decide whether the MVP ships concrete models, abstract base models, or service adapters first, and document rejected alternatives.
Map each persisted field to the service-layer security invariant it supports.
Add or update tests/checks that validate the design artifact is present and references the relevant invariants.
acceptance_criteria: The design note names the exact persisted state needed for TOTP replay prevention and HOTP counter advancement without storing raw secrets or OTP submissions.
The design documents transaction/locking expectations for verification state updates before model code is added.
A local validation check or focused test fails if the persistence design document is removed or no longer references the required controls.
tasks.completion_summary: Added the Django MFA device persistence contract design covering concrete model direction, required TOTP/HOTP persisted state, audit boundaries, transaction locking expectations, and a local design-doc validation test.

tasks.title: Implement Django MFA device models and migrations
tasks.description: Add Django model storage for TOTP and HOTP devices using the persistence contract from 5.1.
steps: Create model classes for TOTP and HOTP device state with encrypted persisted_secret fields, user ownership, confirmation state, timestamps, and the replay/counter fields identified in the design.
Add migrations without embedding real secrets, OTPs, or production-looking fixture values.
Add model-level helpers or constraints that prevent negative counters and preserve per-user device identity.
Add focused model tests for secret non-disclosure, default state, constraints, and migration importability.
acceptance_criteria: TOTP model state can persist encrypted secrets and last_accepted_timecode without exposing raw secret material.
HOTP model state can persist encrypted secrets and hotp_counter with non-negative counter enforcement.
The Django test suite covers model creation, validation constraints, and migrations with synthetic data only.
tasks.completion_summary: Added concrete TOTPDevice and HOTPDevice models, an initial Django migration, encrypted-secret validation, HOTP non-negative counter enforcement, and database-backed model/migration tests.

tasks.title: Add atomic device enrollment and verification adapters
tasks.description: Wrap the service primitives with Django model adapters that perform enrollment and verification state transitions atomically.
steps: Add enrollment adapters that create unconfirmed device records from TOTP and HOTP enrollment results.
Add TOTP verification adapter that locks the device row, calls verify_totp, and persists last_accepted_timecode only on success.
Add HOTP verification and resynchronization adapters that lock the device row and persist hotp_counter only on accepted results.
Add concurrency-focused tests proving failed, replayed, and invalid attempts do not advance persisted state.
acceptance_criteria: Accepted TOTP verification updates replay state exactly once and replayed codes remain rejected.
Accepted HOTP verification/resync updates the counter exactly once and failed attempts leave the counter unchanged.
State-changing adapters use a documented transaction/locking boundary and are covered by Django database tests.
tasks.completion_summary: Added atomic model-backed TOTP/HOTP enrollment, verification, and HOTP resynchronization adapters using transaction.atomic and select_for_update, with tests for accepted, failed, replayed, unconfirmed, and resync state transitions.

tasks.title: Integrate throttling and lockout controls for MFA attempts
tasks.description: Provide a local Django throttling boundary for MFA verification attempts so callers do not have to invent rate limiting around OTP checks.
steps: Design a small throttle API backed by Django cache or another existing Django primitive, with caller-controlled scope keys for users/devices.
Apply throttling before expensive OTP verification while avoiding storage of raw submitted OTPs.
Return typed throttle/lockout outcomes that integration code can distinguish from invalid OTP results.
Add tests for allowed attempts, blocked attempts, reset behavior, and no raw OTP leakage in throttle keys or values.
acceptance_criteria: Verification adapters can be configured to reject over-limit attempts before running TOTP/HOTP HMAC loops.
Throttle state never stores raw OTP submissions, raw secrets, or production credentials.
Focused tests cover success, failure, lockout, reset, and interaction with replay/counter state.
tasks.completion_summary: Added a Django-cache-backed MFA throttle API with hashed scope keys, integrated optional throttle checks into TOTP/HOTP model adapters before OTP verification, returned typed throttled outcomes, reset counters on success, and covered lockout/reset/no-OTP-leakage behavior with tests.

tasks.title: Add session elevation helpers for post-MFA state
tasks.description: Provide Django helpers for marking and checking post-MFA session elevation without weakening Django session or CSRF defaults.
steps: Design the session keys, timestamp handling, and invalidation expectations for pre-MFA and post-MFA authentication state.
Add helpers that mark MFA completion, check freshness, and clear elevation on logout or explicit reset.
Add local Django test-client tests for pre-MFA denial, post-MFA access, stale elevation rejection, and logout cleanup.
Document integration expectations without disabling Django authentication middleware, CSRF, password handling, or secure cookie defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Session elevation is represented as a separate boundary from password authentication and can expire independently.
Tests prove protected flows reject pre-MFA sessions and stale post-MFA sessions.
The implementation does not weaken Django security defaults or require applications to disable middleware protections.
tasks.completion_summary: Added session elevation helpers for marking, checking, expiring, clearing, and requiring post-MFA state in Django sessions; documented integration expectations; and added test-client coverage for pre-MFA denial, post-MFA access, stale elevation, explicit reset, and logout cleanup.

tasks.title: Add local Django integration verification checks
tasks.description: Extend the fixture-bound verification model to cover persisted devices, throttling, and session elevation using Django test clients and synthetic users.
steps: Extend django_mfa_toolkit.security_invariants with controls for persisted device state, throttling/lockout, and session elevation.
Add pytest fixtures for synthetic users, synthetic persisted TOTP/HOTP devices, and local protected views.
Add in-process checks proving MFA enforcement, throttle enforcement, replay rejection, and session elevation boundaries.
Document that the checks remain local and do not accept target URLs, hosts, credentials, or arbitrary payloads.
acceptance_criteria: Security invariant tests cover persisted-device verification, throttling, and session elevation with local fixtures only.
No verification helper accepts arbitrary network targets, credentials, or payload lists.
The integration guide points developers and agents to the new local checks.
tasks.completion_summary: Added local Django integration verification checks for persisted TOTP/HOTP devices, throttle lockout, replay rejection, HOTP resync lockout, and session elevation boundaries using synthetic users and Django test clients; extended the static security invariant registry and updated the integration guide to point to the new local checks.

tasks.title: Document end-to-end Django MFA integration path
tasks.description: Update the agent-followable guide with the completed model, adapter, throttling, and session-elevation integration path.
steps: Update the MVP integration guide to show model-backed TOTP and HOTP enrollment, verification, and resynchronization flows.
Add examples for throttle configuration, post-MFA session elevation, and local verification commands.
Document operational guidance for audit records, lockout handling, key rotation, and avoiding logged OTP/secret material.
Run documentation-adjacent tests or smoke checks that validate referenced modules and commands still exist.
acceptance_criteria: The guide describes a complete local Django integration path from enrollment through protected post-MFA access.
The guide includes exact validation commands for each integration layer.
Documentation examples use pseudo values only and do not contain raw secrets, OTPs, or production credentials.
tasks.completion_summary: Updated the MVP integration guide with model-backed TOTP/HOTP enrollment, confirmation, verification, HOTP resynchronization, throttling, session elevation, operational guidance, and exact validation commands for each integration layer; added documentation coverage for the end-to-end Django path.

tasks.title: Design Django MFA audit persistence contracts
tasks.description: Define Django audit persistence for HOTP verification and resynchronization outcomes while preserving the no-OTP/no-raw-secret boundary.
steps: Define audit model fields for HOTP verification and HOTP resynchronization outcomes.
Map fields to HOTPAuditRecord and HOTPResyncAuditRecord.
Document fields that must not be persisted, including submitted OTPs, raw secrets, and provisioning URIs.
Specify retention, user/device references, migration expectations, and implementation tests.
acceptance_criteria: Design note defines audit persistence without storing OTPs or raw secrets.
The model contract covers success, invalid, replay, throttled, counter-window, resync success, and excessive-drift outcomes.
Expected implementation tests are listed.
tasks.completion_summary: Added an audit persistence contract for HOTP verification and resynchronization events, including model field shape, service-record mappings, classification coverage, retention/deletion guidance, transaction expectations, forbidden persisted data, and required implementation tests; added documentation coverage for the contract.

tasks.title: Implement Django MFA audit event models
tasks.description: Add Django models, migrations, and focused tests for persisting MFA audit events without secret or OTP material.
steps: Add audit event model and migration for HOTP verification and resynchronization metadata.
Add persistence helpers that accept HOTPAuditRecord and HOTPResyncAuditRecord values.
Keep submitted OTPs, raw secrets, provisioning URIs, and production credentials out of model fields and fixtures.
Add model, migration, and non-disclosure tests.
acceptance_criteria: Audit events can be persisted for HOTP verification and resynchronization.
Audit rows contain classification and counter metadata but no OTP material.
Tests prove no raw OTP or secret persistence.
tasks.completion_summary: Added an MFAAuditEvent model and migration for HOTP verification and resynchronization audit metadata, persistence helpers for HOTPAuditRecord and HOTPResyncAuditRecord values, retention behavior through nullable SET_NULL relationships, and focused tests for field mapping, classification coverage, migration importability, and no OTP/secret/provisioning URI persistence.

tasks.title: Integrate optional audit persistence into device adapters
tasks.description: Allow model-backed HOTP verification and resynchronization adapters to persist audit outcomes while preserving atomic counter behavior.
steps: Add optional audit persistence hooks to verify_hotp_device and resync_hotp_device.
Preserve existing return values and backward-compatible behavior when persistence is not requested.
Define and test audit persistence failure behavior.
Add adapter tests for success, invalid, replay, throttled, and resync outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Device adapters can persist audit records locally.
Counter updates remain atomic and correct.
Failed, replayed, throttled, and resync attempts are auditable without storing submitted OTPs.
tasks.completion_summary: Added opt-in HOTP audit persistence to verify_hotp_device and resync_hotp_device with persist_audit=True, preserving default behavior and existing return values; audit rows are created inside the adapter transaction so persistence failures roll back accepted counter updates; added adapter coverage for success, invalid, replay, throttled, resync, no-OTP persistence, default no-audit behavior, and rollback semantics.

tasks.title: Design machine-readable control graph for MFA safeguards
tasks.description: Extend the flat invariant representation into explicit control relationships and compensating-control reasoning.
steps: Design graph nodes and edges for MFA safeguards and acceptable compensating controls.
Represent dependencies such as verification requiring throttling or a documented compensating control.
Include audit persistence, session elevation, replay prevention, and secret storage controls.
Document how agents should interpret missing controls and proposed remedies.
acceptance_criteria: Control relationships are machine-readable.
Compensating-control logic is represented explicitly.
Existing invariant tests continue to pass or have a documented migration path.
tasks.completion_summary: Added a machine-readable MFA control graph with control, implementation, verification, and compensating-control nodes; represented requires, implemented-by, verified-by, and satisfied-by-any relationships for secret storage, replay prevention, throttling or documented lockout, audit persistence, session elevation, and local verification; documented agent interpretation rules and preserved existing invariant APIs and tests.

tasks.title: Add local integration check helpers for downstream projects
tasks.description: Package reusable fixture-bound pytest helpers or Django test mixins for downstream projects to validate MFA integration behavior locally.
steps: Provide a pytest helper or Django test mixin for fixture-bound MFA checks.
Keep helper APIs non-targetable, with no URL, host, credential, or payload-list parameters.
Add tests proving helpers run against synthetic users, synthetic devices, and in-process Django clients only.
Document helper usage and exact validation commands in the integration guide.
acceptance_criteria: Downstream projects can reuse local MFA verification checks.
Helper APIs cannot be repointed at arbitrary targets.
Documentation shows exact test commands.

tasks.title: Design recovery code support
tasks.description: Define recovery-code storage, verification, throttling, audit, and session-elevation expectations before implementation.
steps: Define recovery-code generation, display, storage, and reset boundaries.
Specify hashing or encryption, constant-time comparison, one-time use, replay prevention, throttling, and audit expectations.
Document session elevation behavior after recovery-code verification.
Decide whether recovery codes are in post-MVP implementation scope and split follow-up tasks if accepted.
acceptance_criteria: Design covers constant-time comparison, one-time use, replay prevention, throttling, and audit logging.
No raw recovery codes are stored.
Implementation tasks can be derived directly from the design.

tasks.title: Evaluate django-otp compatibility adapter
tasks.description: Revisit the deferred django-otp compatibility decision now that the MVP service contracts and verification workflow are tested.
steps: Compare current custom model guarantees against django-otp extension points.
Identify whether adapters can preserve encrypted storage, replay prevention, audit semantics, throttling, and HOTP resynchronization.
Document a recommendation: adapter, no adapter, or deferred.
If an adapter is recommended, split explicit implementation tasks and dependency-review tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Decision note states whether django-otp compatibility is worth pursuing.
Security invariants that would be weakened or preserved are explicit.
No runtime dependency is added without a follow-up implementation task.
